401.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu 'Asib, the freed slave of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Jibril (peace be upon him) came to me with fever and plague. I kept the fever in Medina and sent the plague to Syria. Plague is martyrdom for my ummah and a mercy to them, but a torment upon the disbelievers.'
401.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu 'Asib, mantan budak Rasulullah ﷺ (radhiyallahu 'anhu), bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Jibril (alaihissalam) datang kepadaku membawa demam dan wabah penyakit. Aku menahan demam di Madinah dan mengirimkan wabah ke Syam. Wabah adalah syahadah bagi umatku dan rahmat bagi mereka, tetapi siksaan bagi orang-orang kafir.'

402.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu 'Asib (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ went out one night and passed by him and called him, and he came out. Then he passed by Abu Bakr and called him, and he came out. Then he passed by 'Umar and called him, and he came out. They walked until they entered an orchard belonging to one of the Ansar. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to the owner: 'Feed us some unripe dates.' He brought a cluster and the Prophet ate from it, as did Abu Bakr and 'Umar. Then he gave them water to drink.
402.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu 'Asib (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ keluar pada suatu malam dan melewatinya lalu memanggilnya, dan ia keluar. Kemudian beliau melewati Abu Bakr dan memanggilnya, dan ia keluar. Kemudian beliau melewati 'Umar dan memanggilnya, dan ia keluar. Mereka berjalan hingga masuk ke sebuah kebun milik salah seorang Anshar. Rasulullah ﷺ berkata kepada pemiliknya: 'Berilah kami kurma muda.' Ia membawa setandan kurma dan Nabi makan darinya, begitu pula Abu Bakr dan 'Umar. Kemudian beliau memberi mereka air minum.

403.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Khashkhash al-'Anbari (may Allah be pleased with him) that he came to the Prophet ﷺ with his son. The Prophet said: 'Your son?' He said: 'Yes.' He said: 'He will not be held accountable for your crimes, nor will you be held accountable for his.'
403.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Khashkhash al-'Anbari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia datang kepada Nabi ﷺ bersama putranya. Nabi bersabda: 'Anakmu?' Ia berkata: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Ia tidak akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas kejahatanmu, dan kamu tidak akan dimintai pertanggungjawaban atas kejahatannya.'

404.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'You see this old man — he means himself — I spoke with the Prophet of Allah ﷺ and ate with him. I saw the mark between his shoulders on the edge of his left shoulder blade, like a clenched fist — meaning the closed hand — with moles around it like the moles of a cluster of dates.'
404.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Kalian melihat orang tua ini — ia maksudkan dirinya sendiri — aku pernah berbicara dengan Nabi Allah ﷺ dan makan bersamanya. Aku melihat tanda di antara kedua bahunya di tepi tulang belikat kirinya, seperti kepalan tangan — maksudnya tangan yang terkepal — dengan tahi lalat di sekelilingnya seperti tahi lalat setandan kurma.'

405.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Prophet ﷺ set out on a journey, he would say: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardship of travel, from the distress of return, from regressing after progress, from the supplication of the oppressed, and from seeing a bad situation in one's family and wealth.'
405.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika Nabi ﷺ berangkat dalam sebuah perjalanan, beliau berdoa: 'Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kesusahan perjalanan, dari kesedihan saat kembali, dari kemunduran setelah kemajuan, dari doa orang yang dizalimi, dan dari melihat keadaan buruk pada keluarga dan harta.'

406.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to say when he traveled: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardship of travel, from the distress of return, from regressing after progress, from the supplication of the oppressed, and from seeing a bad situation in one's family and wealth.'
406.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa berdoa ketika bepergian: 'Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kesusahan perjalanan, dari kesedihan saat kembali, dari kemunduran setelah kemajuan, dari doa orang yang dizalimi, dan dari melihat keadaan buruk pada keluarga dan harta.'

407.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when he traveled used to say: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardship of travel, from the distress of return, from regressing after progress, from the supplication of the oppressed, and from seeing a bad situation in one's family and wealth.'
407.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ketika bepergian biasa berdoa: 'Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kesusahan perjalanan, dari kesedihan saat kembali, dari kemunduran setelah kemajuan, dari doa orang yang dizalimi, dan dari melihat keadaan buruk pada keluarga dan harta.'

408.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that he saw the Seal between the shoulders of the Prophet ﷺ. He had seen the Prophet ﷺ but was not formally counted among his companions.
408.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia melihat Stempel di antara kedua bahu Nabi ﷺ. Ia telah melihat Nabi ﷺ tetapi tidak secara resmi dihitung di antara para sahabatnya.

409.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Let none of you urinate in a hole. When you sleep, extinguish the lamp, for a mouse may take the wick and set the household on fire. Tie up your waterskins, cover your drinks, close your doors at night.'
409.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah salah seorang dari kalian kencing di lubang. Ketika kalian tidur, padamkan lampu, karena seekor tikus mungkin mengambil sumbunya dan membakar penghuni rumah. Ikatlah kantong air kalian, tutuplah minuman kalian, kuncilah pintu-pintu kalian pada malam hari.'

410.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when he set out on a journey used to say: 'O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardship of travel, from the distress of return, from regressing after progress, from the supplication of the oppressed, and from seeing a bad situation in one's family and wealth.'
410.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ketika berangkat dalam sebuah perjalanan biasa berdoa: 'Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kesusahan perjalanan, dari kesedihan saat kembali, dari kemunduran setelah kemajuan, dari doa orang yang dizalimi, dan dari melihat keadaan buruk pada keluarga dan harta.'

411.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that the iqama was called for Fajr prayer, and the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw a man praying the two rak'ahs of the dawn sunnah. He said to him: 'Which of your two prayers do you count? The one you prayed alone or the one you prayed with us?'
411.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa iqamah dikumandangkan untuk shalat Subuh, dan Rasulullah ﷺ melihat seorang laki-laki sedang menunaikan dua rakaat sunnah fajar. Beliau berkata kepadanya: 'Shalat mana yang kamu hitung pahalanya? Yang kamu kerjakan sendiri atau yang kamu kerjakan bersama kami?'

412.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that he came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and ate with him from his food. He then said: 'May Allah forgive you, O Messenger of Allah' — meaning he asked for forgiveness for him. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'And for you' — and then recited: 'And seek forgiveness for your sin and for the believing men and believing women.' He then looked at the Seal of Prophethood.
412.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan makan bersamanya dari makanannya. Ia kemudian berkata: 'Semoga Allah mengampunimu, wahai Rasulullah' — yakni ia memohonkan ampunan untuknya. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Dan untukmu' — kemudian membaca: 'Dan mohonlah ampun atas dosamu dan bagi orang-orang mukmin laki-laki dan perempuan.' Ia kemudian melihat Stempel Kenabian.

413.iwh_en: It was narrated through 'Asim al-Ahwal that 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis had seen the Messenger of Allah ﷺ but was not formally counted among his companions.
413.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan melalui 'Ashim al-Ahwal bahwa 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis pernah melihat Rasulullah ﷺ tetapi tidak secara resmi dihitung di antara para sahabatnya.

414.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'I saw the Prophet ﷺ, entered upon him, ate from his food, drank from his drink, and saw the Seal of Prophethood on the edge of his left shoulder blade — like a gathered cluster with moles around it.'
414.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku melihat Nabi ﷺ, masuk ke hadapannya, makan dari makanannya, minum dari minumannya, dan melihat Stempel Kenabian di tepi tulang belikat kirinya — seperti tandan yang terkumpul dengan tahi lalat di sekelilingnya.'

415.iwh_en: It was narrated from 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when he traveled used to say: 'O Allah, You are the Companion in travel and the Successor in the family. O Allah, accompany us in our travel and be our Successor in our family. O Allah, I seek refuge in You from the hardship of travel, from the distress of return, and from regressing after progress.'
415.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari 'Abdullah ibn Sarjis (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ketika bepergian biasa berdoa: 'Ya Allah, Engkaulah Teman dalam perjalanan dan Pengganti bagi keluarga. Ya Allah, temanilah kami dalam perjalanan kami dan jadilah Pengganti kami bagi keluarga kami. Ya Allah, aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kesusahan perjalanan, dari kesedihan saat kembali, dan dari kemunduran setelah kemajuan.'

416.iwh_en: It was narrated from a woman named Raja' that she was with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ when a woman came to him with her son and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, pray to Allah for me to bless him, for three of my children have died.' The Messenger of Allah ﷺ asked: 'Since you became Muslim?' She said: 'Yes.' He said: 'Then you are well guarded against the Fire through them.'
416.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita bernama Raja' bahwa ia sedang bersama Rasulullah ﷺ ketika seorang wanita datang kepadanya bersama putranya dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, doakanlah aku kepada Allah agar memberkahi dia, karena tiga orang anakku telah meninggal.' Rasulullah ﷺ bertanya: 'Sejak engkau masuk Islam?' Ia berkata: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Maka engkau terlindungi dengan baik dari api neraka melalui mereka.'

417.iwh_en: It was narrated from a woman named Mawiyya, who had lost several children, through a companion of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, that a woman came to the Prophet ﷺ with her son and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, pray to Allah for me to bless him, for three of my children have died.' He said: 'Three of them?' She said: 'Yes.' He said: 'You have indeed a strong shield against the Fire.'
417.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari seorang wanita bernama Mawiyya, yang telah kehilangan beberapa orang anak, melalui seorang sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ, bahwa seorang wanita datang kepada Nabi ﷺ bersama putranya dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, doakanlah aku kepada Allah agar memberkahi dia, karena tiga orang anakku telah meninggal.' Beliau bersabda: 'Tiga dari mereka?' Ia berkata: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Sungguh engkau memiliki perisai yang kuat dari api neraka.'

418.iwh_en: It was narrated from Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyya, the carrier of good tidings for the Messenger of Allah ﷺ (may Allah be pleased with him), that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ saw a man walking between the graves in leather sandals and said: 'O wearer of the smooth sandals, take them off.'
418.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyya, pembawa kabar gembira bagi Rasulullah ﷺ (radhiyallahu 'anhu), bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ melihat seorang laki-laki berjalan di antara kuburan dengan sandal kulit dan bersabda: 'Wahai pemakai sandal halus, lepaskanlah keduanya.'

419.iwh_en: It was narrated from Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyya (may Allah be pleased with him) through a man of Banu Sadus named Daysam, that he said to Bashir: 'We have neighbors from Banu Tamim who steal from our distant properties whenever we are absent, yet things from their wealth come to us.' The Prophet ﷺ told him to take what comes to you by legitimate means and leave what is unlawful.
419.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) melalui seorang laki-laki dari Banu Sadus bernama Daysam, bahwa ia berkata kepada Bashir: 'Kami memiliki tetangga dari Banu Tamim yang mencuri dari harta kami yang jauh setiap kali kami tidak ada, namun barang-barang dari harta mereka datang kepada kami.' Nabi ﷺ memberitahunya untuk mengambil apa yang datang kepadanya dengan cara yang sah dan meninggalkan yang tidak halal.

420.iwh_en: It was narrated from Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyya (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was walking with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ holding his hand. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to him: 'O son of al-Khasasiyya, what do you find yourself objecting to in Allah, Blessed and Exalted? This morning you walk alongside His Messenger.'
420.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Bashir ibn al-Khasasiyya (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia sedang berjalan bersama Rasulullah ﷺ sambil memegang tangannya. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepadanya: 'Wahai putra al-Khasasiyya, apa yang kamu temukan dirimu keberatan kepada Allah Yang Maha Berkah lagi Maha Tinggi? Pagi ini kamu berjalan di sisi Rasul-Nya.'

421.iwh_en: It was narrated from the sister of a woman who participated in six expeditions with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ that they said: 'We used to tend to the wounded, nurse the sick, and care for those who needed help.' She mentioned that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded that women who went on expeditions should be given a portion of any war gains.
421.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari saudari seorang wanita yang ikut dalam enam ekspedisi bersama Rasulullah ﷺ bahwa mereka berkata: 'Kami biasa merawat yang terluka, merawat yang sakit, dan merawat mereka yang membutuhkan bantuan.' Ia menyebutkan bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan agar wanita-wanita yang ikut dalam ekspedisi diberi bagian dari harta rampasan perang.

422.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to them while they were washing his daughter. He said: 'Wash her three times, or five, or more than that if you see fit, with water and lote-leaves. Place camphor or something of camphor in the last washing. When you are done, inform me.' When they finished, they informed him and he gave them his waist-wrapper and said: 'Wrap her in it.'
422.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ datang kepada mereka ketika mereka sedang memandikan putrinya. Beliau bersabda: 'Mandikan ia tiga kali, atau lima, atau lebih dari itu jika kalian rasa perlu, dengan air dan daun bidara. Letakkan kapur barus atau sesuatu dari kapur barus pada bilasan terakhir. Ketika kalian selesai, beritahu aku.' Ketika mereka selesai, mereka memberitahu beliau dan beliau memberikan kain sarungnya sambil bersabda: 'Bungkuslah ia dengannya.'

423.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that among the conditions the Messenger of Allah ﷺ took from them at the pledge of allegiance was that they would not wail in mourning. She said: 'None of us fulfilled it except five women.'
423.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa di antara syarat-syarat yang Rasulullah ﷺ ambil dari mereka saat baiat adalah bahwa mereka tidak akan meratap. Ia berkata: 'Tidak ada di antara kami yang memenuhinya kecuali lima wanita.'

424.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'I participated in seven expeditions with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, staying behind to guard their belongings, prepare food for them, tend to the sick, and treat the wounded.'
424.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia berkata: 'Aku berpartisipasi dalam tujuh ekspedisi bersama Rasulullah ﷺ, tinggal di belakang untuk menjaga barang-barang mereka, mempersiapkan makanan untuk mereka, merawat yang sakit, dan mengobati yang terluka.'

425.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — may my father and mother be sacrificed for him — commanded them to bring out on Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha the unmarried girls, those in seclusion, and the menstruating women. The menstruating women were to stay away from the prayer area but witness the goodness and the supplication of the Muslims. Someone asked: 'What about one who has no outer garment?' He said: 'Let her sister lend her one of her outer garments.'
425.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ — semoga ayah dan ibuku menjadi tebusan beliau — memerintahkan mereka untuk mengeluarkan pada Idul Fitri dan Idul Adha gadis-gadis yang belum menikah, mereka yang berada dalam pengasingan, dan wanita-wanita yang sedang haid. Wanita-wanita yang haid diperintahkan menjauh dari tempat shalat namun tetap menyaksikan kebaikan dan doa kaum Muslim. Seseorang bertanya: 'Bagaimana dengan yang tidak memiliki jilbab?' Beliau bersabda: 'Hendaklah saudarinya meminjamkan salah satu jilbabnya.'

426.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded them to bring out the unmarried girls, those in seclusion, and the menstruating women on the two Eids so they could witness the goodness and the supplication of the Muslims.
426.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan mereka untuk mengeluarkan gadis-gadis yang belum menikah, mereka yang berada dalam pengasingan, dan wanita-wanita yang sedang haid pada dua hari Eid agar mereka dapat menyaksikan kebaikan dan doa kaum Muslim.

427.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that when Zaynab, the daughter of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, died, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said to them: 'Wash her an odd number of times — three or five — and place camphor or something of camphor in the fifth. When you have washed her, inform me.' When they informed him, he gave them his waist-wrapper and said: 'Wrap her in it close to her body.'
427.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ketika Zaynab, putri Rasulullah ﷺ, meninggal, Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda kepada mereka: 'Mandikan ia dengan bilangan ganjil — tiga atau lima — dan letakkan kapur barus atau sesuatu dari kapur barus pada bilasan kelima. Ketika kalian telah memandikannya, beritahu aku.' Ketika mereka memberitahu beliau, beliau memberikan kain sarungnya dan bersabda: 'Bungkuslah ia dengannya dekat dengan tubuhnya.'

428.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that when the verse was revealed — 'that they pledge allegiance to you that they will not associate anything with Allah... and that they will not disobey you in what is right' — wailing in mourning was among the things forbidden. She said: 'I said: O Messenger of Allah, except the family of so-and-so, for they helped me in the pre-Islamic period and I must reciprocate.' He said: 'Except the family of so-and-so.'
428.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ketika ayat ini turun — 'bahwa mereka berbaiat kepadamu untuk tidak menyekutukan Allah dengan sesuatu apapun... dan tidak mendurhakai engkau dalam perkara yang baik' — meratap dalam berkabung termasuk dalam hal-hal yang dilarang. Ia berkata: 'Aku berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, kecuali keluarga si Fulan, karena mereka telah membantuku di masa Jahiliyah dan aku harus membalas budi.' Beliau bersabda: 'Kecuali keluarga si Fulan.'

429.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that when the Messenger of Allah ﷺ came to Medina, he gathered the women of the Ansar in a house and sent 'Umar ibn al-Khattab to them. He stood at the door, greeted them, and they returned the greeting. He then conveyed the message of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ to them.
429.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ketika Rasulullah ﷺ tiba di Madinah, beliau mengumpulkan wanita-wanita Anshar di sebuah rumah dan mengutus 'Umar ibn al-Khattab kepada mereka. Ia berdiri di pintu, mengucapkan salam, dan mereka membalas salamnya. Ia kemudian menyampaikan pesan Rasulullah ﷺ kepada mereka.

430.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that she was among those who pledged allegiance to the Prophet ﷺ, and among what he required from them was that they would not wail in mourning and would not speak with men except their mahrams.
430.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia termasuk di antara mereka yang berbaiat kepada Nabi ﷺ, dan di antara yang beliau wajibkan dari mereka adalah bahwa mereka tidak akan meratap dan tidak berbicara dengan laki-laki kecuali mahram mereka.

431.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to command them to bring out the unmarried girls, the menstruating women, and those in seclusion on the Eids. The menstruating women were to stay away from the prayer area but witness the goodness and the supplication together with the Muslims.
431.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa memerintahkan mereka untuk mengeluarkan gadis-gadis yang belum menikah, wanita yang haid, dan mereka yang berada dalam pengasingan pada hari-hari Eid. Wanita yang haid diperintahkan menjauh dari tempat shalat namun menyaksikan kebaikan dan doa bersama kaum Muslim.

432.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that she washed a daughter of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He commanded them to wash her with lote-leaves three times. If that was sufficient, well; otherwise five; if that was sufficient, well; otherwise more. She said: 'We considered that more than that would be seven.'
432.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa ia memandikan seorang putri Rasulullah ﷺ. Beliau memerintahkan mereka untuk memandikannya dengan daun bidara tiga kali. Jika itu mencukupi, maka cukup; jika tidak, maka lima kali; jika mencukupi, maka cukup; jika tidak, maka lebih dari itu. Ia berkata: 'Kami berpendapat bahwa lebih dari itu adalah tujuh kali.'

433.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm 'Atiyya al-Ansariyya (may Allah be pleased with her) that one of the daughters of the Prophet ﷺ died, and he commanded them to wash her three or five times or more if they saw fit, with water and lote-leaves, and to use camphor in the last washing.
433.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm 'Atiyya al-Anshariyya (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa salah seorang putri Nabi ﷺ meninggal, dan beliau memerintahkan mereka untuk memandikannya tiga atau lima kali atau lebih jika mereka rasa perlu, dengan air dan daun bidara, dan menggunakan kapur barus pada bilasan terakhir.

434.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Before the Hour there will be great liars.'
434.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sebelum Kiamat akan ada para pembohong besar.'

435.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that Ma'iz ibn Malik — a short man wearing only a lower garment with no upper wrap — was brought before the Prophet ﷺ. He had confessed to fornication. The Prophet turned away from him again and again, but Ma'iz persisted until he had confessed four times, so the Prophet ordered him to be stoned.
435.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Ma'iz ibn Malik — seorang laki-laki pendek yang hanya mengenakan kain bawah tanpa kain atas — dibawa ke hadapan Nabi ﷺ. Ia telah mengakui zina. Nabi berpaling darinya berulang kali, tetapi Ma'iz terus bersikeras hingga ia mengaku empat kali, maka Nabi memerintahkan agar ia dirajam.

436.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that the muezzin of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to call the adhan and then wait without calling the iqama until he saw the Messenger of Allah ﷺ come out. When he saw him, he would call the iqama.
436.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa muazzin Rasulullah ﷺ biasa mengumandangkan adzan kemudian menunggu tanpa mengumandangkan iqamah hingga ia melihat Rasulullah ﷺ keluar. Ketika ia melihat beliau, ia mengumandangkan iqamah.

437.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'This religion will remain standing until there have been twelve caliphs from Quraysh. Then false claimants will emerge before the Hour, then a group from my ummah will fight for truth until the Hour comes.'
437.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Agama ini akan tetap berdiri hingga ada dua belas khalifah dari Quraisy. Kemudian para pendusta akan muncul sebelum Kiamat, kemudian sekelompok dari umatku akan berperang demi kebenaran hingga Kiamat tiba.'

438.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that when they prayed behind the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, they used to say the salam gesturing with their hands to the right and left. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'What is the matter with people gesturing with their hands like the tails of restless horses? It is enough for one of you to say it calmly while gesturing with his hand toward his companion.'
438.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika mereka shalat di belakang Rasulullah ﷺ, mereka biasa mengucapkan salam dengan menggerakkan tangan ke kanan dan ke kiri. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ada apa dengan orang-orang yang menggerakkan tangan mereka seperti ekor kuda yang gelisah? Cukup bagi salah seorang dari kalian mengucapkannya dengan tenang sambil memberi isyarat dengan tangannya ke arah temannya.'

439.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was asked about the grey hair of the Prophet ﷺ. He said: 'He had some grey hairs on his head which, when he oiled his hair, would not be visible, but when he did not oil it, they would show.'
439.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia ditanya tentang uban Nabi ﷺ. Ia berkata: 'Beliau memiliki beberapa helai rambut putih di kepalanya yang, ketika beliau meminyaki rambutnya, tidak terlihat, tetapi ketika beliau tidak meminyakinya, akan tampak.'

440.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to recite in the Dhuhr prayer Surah al-A'la and its like, and in the Fajr prayer something longer than that.
440.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa membaca dalam shalat Dhuhur Surah al-A'la dan yang semisalnya, dan dalam shalat Subuh sesuatu yang lebih panjang dari itu.

441.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Seek the Night of Power in the last ten nights.'
441.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Carilah Lailatul Qadar pada sepuluh malam terakhir.'

442.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that when asked whether he used to sit in gatherings with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, he said: 'Yes. He was long in silence and rarely laughed. His companions would recite poetry and speak of their affairs in his presence and they would laugh, and he would sometimes smile.'
442.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika ditanya apakah ia pernah duduk dalam majelis bersama Rasulullah ﷺ, ia berkata: 'Ya. Beliau lama diam dan jarang tertawa. Para sahabatnya membacakan syair dan membicarakan urusan mereka di hadapannya dan mereka tertawa, sementara beliau terkadang tersenyum.'

443.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man asked the Prophet ﷺ: 'Must I perform ablution after eating camel meat?' He said: 'Yes.' He asked: 'Must I perform ablution after eating sheep meat?' He said: 'If you wish.'
443.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki bertanya kepada Nabi ﷺ: 'Apakah aku harus berwudhu setelah makan daging unta?' Beliau bersabda: 'Ya.' Ia bertanya: 'Apakah aku harus berwudhu setelah makan daging domba?' Beliau bersabda: 'Jika kamu mau.'

444.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ had eyes with a slight redness in them and a lean heel.
444.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memiliki mata dengan sedikit warna kemerahan di dalamnya dan tumit yang ramping.

445.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to deliver the sermon standing, would sit between the two portions of the sermon, would recite some verses, and would remind people.
445.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa menyampaikan khutbah sambil berdiri, duduk di antara dua bagian khutbah, membacakan beberapa ayat, dan mengingatkan orang-orang.

446.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura al-Suwwa'i (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say in the Farewell Pilgrimage: 'This religion will always be dominant over those who oppose it. No opponent or deserter can harm it until there pass from my ummah twelve commanders' — then his voice became faint saying the rest.
446.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura al-Suwwa'i (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda dalam Haji Wada': 'Agama ini akan selalu unggul atas mereka yang menentangnya. Tidak ada penentang atau pembelot yang dapat merugikannya hingga berlalu dari umatku dua belas komandan' — kemudian suaranya melemah ketika mengucapkan sisanya.

447.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that a household at al-Harra was in need and a she-camel died among them. The Prophet ﷺ gave them permission to eat it. He said: The rest of it sustained them.
447.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa sebuah rumah tangga di al-Harrah sedang dalam kesulitan dan seekor unta betina mati di antara mereka. Nabi ﷺ memberi mereka izin untuk memakannya. Ia berkata: Sisanya menopang mereka.

448.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man died during the time of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. A man came to him and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, so-and-so has died.' He said: 'He has not died.' The man came a second and third time to inform him. The Prophet ﷺ asked: 'How did he die?' He said: 'He stabbed himself with a broad arrowhead.' So the Prophet ﷺ did not pray over him.
448.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki meninggal pada masa Rasulullah ﷺ. Seorang laki-laki datang kepada beliau dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, si Fulan telah meninggal.' Beliau bersabda: 'Ia belum meninggal.' Laki-laki itu datang kedua dan ketiga kalinya untuk memberi tahu beliau. Nabi ﷺ bertanya: 'Bagaimana ia meninggal?' Ia berkata: 'Ia menusuk dirinya dengan ujung anak panah yang lebar.' Maka Nabi ﷺ tidak menshalatinya.

449.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura al-Suwwa'i (may Allah be pleased with him) that he heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say in the Farewell Pilgrimage: 'This religion will remain dominant over those who oppose it. No opponent or deserter can harm it until there pass twelve commanders from my ummah' — then his voice faded, and his father said he heard the rest: 'all of them from Quraysh.'
449.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura al-Suwwa'i (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda dalam Haji Wada': 'Agama ini akan tetap unggul atas mereka yang menentangnya. Tidak ada penentang atau pembelot yang dapat merugikannya hingga berlalu dua belas komandan dari umatku' — kemudian suaranya mengecil, dan ayahnya mengatakan ia mendengar sisanya: 'semuanya dari Quraisy.'

450.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir ibn Samura (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was asked how the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to deliver the sermon. He said: 'He used to deliver it standing, though he would sit once, then rise again.'
450.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir ibn Samura (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ia ditanya bagaimana Rasulullah ﷺ biasa menyampaikan khutbah. Ia berkata: 'Beliau biasa menyampaikannya sambil berdiri, meskipun beliau duduk sekali kemudian berdiri lagi.'
